hero.subtitle: I design interfaces in Figma, then build them in React. One person, one vision, no handoff gaps.

focal.label: Auk — Smart Plant Care IoT App

intro: I design interfaces from user empathy, then ship them myself in React. Started as a fullstack engineer building products, realized most handoff friction comes from broken communication between design and code. So I learned design properly — research, Figma, design systems, the full cycle. Now I'm fluent in both worlds.

For the past year I've been taking products from discovery conversations straight to production: defining user flows in Figma, building design systems that engineering actually wants to use, and implementing the interfaces myself to maintain that coherence. That overlap between design intent and code reality is where great products live.

disciplines.title:
Product &
UI/UX Design
disciplines.body: Discovery and user research → wireframes → high-fidelity prototypes in Figma. User flows, information architecture, design systems, component libraries, usability testing, design-to-dev handoff that actually works.

disciplines.title:
Fullstack
Development
disciplines.body: React and Next.js frontends, Node.js and NestJS backends, PostgreSQL and Firebase. Full-cycle shipping: from design specs to APIs to deployed product. 5+ shipped dashboards, e-commerce platforms, and real-time IoT systems.

disciplines.title:
End-to-End
Ownership
disciplines.body: I own the whole thing. Discovery calls with clients, translating requirements into architecture, designing in Figma, building in code, deploying to production. No handoff gaps. No 'that's impossible' conversations.

resume.description: Traditional resume format for recruiters and hiring teams. Two versions available: design-focused or engineering-focused.

contact.intro: Have a project that needs design and development? Looking to understand how design-to-dev handoff actually works? Let's talk. I'm open to full-time roles, contracts, or product collaboration.
